title: Iron Ore Under Pressure Amid Recovering Production and Softening Demand
section_path: Iron Ore Under Pressure Amid Recovering Production and Softening Demand
section_path_text: Iron Ore Under Pressure Amid Recovering Production and Softening Demand
summary:
Main
By Ulf Bergman
After an extensive slide, iron ore prices have found some support at an eighteen-month low. Iron ore with 63.5 per cent iron content for delivery in Tianjin is currently trading at 85 dollars per tonne, a level that was last seen in May 2020 when China embarked on its spectacular V-shaped post-pandemic recovery. The recent travails of the Chinese economy, with softening demand for steel from a sluggish real estate and property sector, and the curbs on the country's steel production have received much of the blame for the bearish sentiment in the iron ore markets.
